summary: This is a brief administrative post sharing a link to a Prezi presentation given at the Joint Mathematics Meetings (JMM). The author notes she found free wifi at the San Antonio airport and uses the opportunity to share her talk. She acknowledges an embarrassing oversight in her presentation: failing to mention math podcasts as a form of public-facing mathematics communication, despite participating in a weekly podcast herself. An audience member who pointed out this gap has agreed to write a guest post surveying math podcasts.
key_points: The author presented at the Joint Mathematics Meetings and is sharing her Prezi slides
She admits to overlooking math podcasts in her discussion of public-facing mathematics
The omission is particularly ironic given her own involvement in a weekly podcast
A follow-up guest post surveying math podcasts is promised
money_quotes: Embarrassing not because I knew about them – I didn't – but because I should have, since after all I participate in a weekly podcast myself, so of course I know it's a new and exciting medium.
key_insight: Even experts in public communication can have blind spots about the very mediums they participate in.
